actual_run_inference: VAL-1: the finite/proxy baseline was confirmed on this run's window.
What was tested: The recovered beta parameter remains pinned at 1/2 across the tested gauge scales.
This preserves the route the experiment witnesses; it does not prove the base claim or any necessary condition.

expected_signature.pass_rule: |recovered_beta - 0.5| within declared tolerance for every tested k
expected_signature.tolerance: adaptive precision tolerance for this run

baseline_hypothesis.plain_statement: The recovered beta parameter remains pinned at 1/2 across the tested gauge scales.
baseline_hypothesis.why_this_matters: Beta stability is the finite-precision proxy for predicate-preservation under the gauge. It is the closest experiment to NC4 (predicate transport).

candidate_lemmas.name: Finite Beta-Stability Lemma
candidate_lemmas.statement: On this run's window, The recovered beta parameter remains pinned at 1/2 across the tested gauge scales held within tolerance (primary metric recovered_beta: observed ≈ 0.5). Formalizing this as a finite/proxy lemma is the next research step.

disallowed_conclusions: beta is invariant at untested k.
The RH predicate transports exactly under the gauge.
intended_inference_if_passed: predicate-preservation proxy is consistent on the tested window
the route to NC4 via beta stability is preserved
this is not a proof of NC4
next_hypotheses: Extend the tested window for VAL-1 to confirm robustness.
Tighten the tolerance on recovered_beta and re-run.

raw_observations.verifier_interpretation: beta_hat=0.500000 within tolerance of 0.5 (drift=0.00e+00, tol=1.00e-10 via dps-derived (dps=80.0)).
